3.7	We recommended and Management agreed that concerned personnel of the Accounting and Treasury Units coordinate and regularly reconcile their records monthly or at least quarterly for an accurate and fair presentation of cash accounts in the financial statement.

4.7	We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office strictly observe compliance with the perpetual inventory method of accounting cited above for the supplies and materials procured for construction projects implemented by administration.  Also, require the Municipal Treasurer, tasked to enforce controls on the receipt and utilization, to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) monthly to the Accounting Office.
5.8	We recommended and Management agreed to undertake all the procedures under COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.

6.9	We recommended and Management agreed to impose proper monitoring and control that facilitate timely review of the appropriated amounts.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure control of expenditures based on the allocation and regularly reconcile with the plan and monitoring prepared and kept by the LDRRM Officer.

7.9	We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers relating to the final payment of infrastructure projects are adequately supported with mandatory documentary requirements before processing the payments.

8.8	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly impose liquidated damages on delayed deliveries of goods procured as prescribed by Annex D, Item 3.1 of Revised IRR of RA 9184 (Updated as of March 31, 2021).
9.7	We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned personnel to prepare the IIRUP and initiate proper disposal to avoid further deterioration of the properties.

10.11	We recommended and the Management agreed that the Sangguniang Bayan, through the Vice-Mayor, revisits the pertinent provisions of Articles 107 and 417 of the IRR of RA 7160.  The Budget Officer and the Planning and Development Officer coordinate with the Local Chief Executive to formulate priority development projects that are procurement-and-implementation-ready to minimize realignments.

11.12	We recommended that Management instruct the MHO to prepare and submit the following documents to support their claims of ADHP and SRA:
List of the eligible HWs determined by the Local Health board;
DTRs/accomplishment report; and
Approved working arrangement indicating the area of assignment.
Also, advise Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation as provided by Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

12.9	We recommended that the Management refrain from entrusting the procurement process to the requesting office for goods not subject to public bidding.  Instead, delegate to the BAC, all procurement activities, may it be public bidding or alternative mode of procurement, to ensure the proper way of procuring based on RA 9184.

13.5	We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also in the procurement of goods.  Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with at least 1% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount, otherwise any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials.

14.10	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Development Council, in close coordination with the MPDC, Municipal Engineer, and the BAC, properly plan the priority development projects of the Municipality by ensuring that programs and projects be procurement-and-implementation-ready before the start of the budget year.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer regularly the funds accruing to the 20% DF upon receipt of IRA to ensure that funds are made available for utilization.

Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare appropriate adjusting entry to record the stallholders' uncollected balance and follow the accrual basis in recording the succeeding revenues.

We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the intra-agency receivables and intra-payables accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances thus ensuring the presentation of reliable date on the financial reports of the LGU as of a given period of time.

We recommended that Management exert extra effort to require employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances and strengthen the internal control on granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that Management immediately implement the projects/activities for an efficient and economical utilization of government funds and to attain the desired objective of the projects.

We recommended that the Management appropriate one percent of the Local Government Unit’s Internal Revenue Allotment as required in Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and to prepare the Local Development Plan and Local Investment Plan for Children with programs, projects and activities that promote and protect the rights of the children and disadvantaged sector of society.

We recommend that Management require the GAD Focal Person to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports /documents mentioned in Section 4 C.8 (5) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated stock card (SC) for each inventory items and property card (PC) for each PPE items, which shall be the bases in preparing the Reports on the physical counts of inventories and Reports on the physical counts of PPEs.

Likewise, direct the inventory committee to conduct immediate physical stocktaking of inventories and physical counts of property, plant and equipments (PPEs).  

If no existing inventory committee, create and inventory committee composed of a Chairman and two (2) members through the issuance of an Office Order, and direct said committee member to conduct an immediate physical counts of inventories and PPEs.
observations.managementAction: One personnel is assigned to update and maintain the stock cards and property card.
observations.reason: Stock cards and Property Cards were not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to require the BAC to prepare regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report (see attached PMR Form) for all procurement activities of the municipality for submission to the GPBB every semester as required under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.  A printed copy of the report shallbe furnished to this Office to allow the audit team to verify the status of procurement activities specified in the Annual Procurement Plan as basis for auditor’s action, if any.
observations.managementAction: BAC is reorganized by the present administration

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal 	Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy between the accounting records 	and the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.

We recommended that management direct the Accountant to require the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) as one of the basic document supporting fund transfers pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001 and to record all fund transfers as receivables to monitor the utilization of the fund and to require the leagues  to submit their liquidation reports on the amounts transferred to them.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant  send follow up  	demand letters requiring the submission of the Fund  Utilization/Liquidation   Report   from   NGOs/POs  with  outstanding  	balances to ascertain that funds granted were utilized in accordance with  	Memorandum  of  Agreement  (MOA).  Moreover, direct the Program  	coordinators of the projects to monitor the compliance per memorandum  	of  agreement to guarantee that the fund transfers were utilized for its  	intended purpose.

We  recommended that  the  Municipal  School  Board   limit the  	utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under and RA 	7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 	1998 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers 	could avail under this fund.

We  recommended  that  management  instruct  the  Municipal  	Treasurer to closely monitor and strictly require the revenue collectors to  	regularly remit intact their collections to prevent the use of public funds 	for their own personal purposes.

We   recommended   that   management   direct  the  Municipal  	Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of Section 7.2.1a of the COA 	Circular No. 2009-006 by submitting disbursement vouchers and financial 	reports within the time frame.

We   recommended that management require the LDRRMO to  	adhere strictly the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated  	Sept. 12, 2012 by submitting the Report on Sources and Utilization of 		LDRRMF to COA within the prescribed  time frame.

We   recommended   that  management  adhere  strictly  to  the  	approved rates embodied in the Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance No. 07-	2010  and  instruct the officers concerned to refund the excess amount  	upon receipt of the Notice of Disallowances.

We   recommended   that  management   direct   the  Municipal  	Accountant  to  prepare  and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation  	Statement to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445  	and COA Circular No. 96-011.
The Municipal accountant should maintain subsidiary ledger for  	each account for easy preparation of the monthly bank reconciliation  	statement. It should be prepared per bank account to establish accurate  	balances of all bank accounts.

We recommend that the Executive department limits the payment  	of    travel  to   Punong   Barangay   since  the   Barangay  has  its  own  	Appropriation  and direct the Accountant  to monitor and meticulously  	review the traveling  claims of the Punong Barangays to avoid possible  	loss of government funds.
We  recommended  that  management  strictly comply with the  	minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dumped  	in accordance to Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003.
We recommend that management ensure the compliance on the  	guidelines for establishment of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in  	accordance to Sections 32 and 33 of RA 9003.
We recommended that management stop immediately the practice 	of spending  government  funds  for  religious  or private purposes being  	violative of Section 335 of Republic Act No. 7160.
We recommend that management adhere strictly to the provisions  	of Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave in the grant of  	monetization to municipal officials and employees.

We recommended that management give priority to address the deficiency and direct the concerned personnel to perform the following:

1.Accounting and Treasury Offices to reconcile the balances of their records of all bank accounts before closing the books to correct any deficiencies noted.

2.Prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-125A dated March 4, 1992 and submit the monthly bank reconciliation to the Office of the Commission on Audit. Failure on the part of the official concerned on the submission would compel us to impose the sanctions under Section 122 of PD 1445.

3.The Office of the Municipal Accountant to perform the Bank Reconciliation and not to delegate said function to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in order to observe appropriate segregation of duties.
observations.managementAction: Reconcile the general ledgers and the cash books of the treasury department. Submit  monthly BRS for all funds.
observations.reason: Submitted Bank reconciliation but  the reconciling items presented in   total amount  not in detailed items, thus the purpose of BRS was not met.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management give priority on the implementation of the following recommendations by issuing office orders to personnel who will perform the task:

Inventory Committee:

1.Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment.
2.Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type showing the balance of property and equipment per cards and per count and the shortage/overage, if any.
3.Prepare the report yearly in three copies, certified correct by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency, and submit to the Office of the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.  

Accounting Office:

1.Reconcile the report of the inventory committee with the control accounts in the general ledger.
2.Determine the discrepancy and effect adjustments in the books, if warranted.
3.Maintain and update regularly the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC).

Municipal Treasurer /Property Officer:
 
1.Prepare the inventory guidelines, indicating the date of physical inventory, who will supervise the count, the personnel to act as counters and checkers, and the sequence of the counting, for approval by the Municipal Mayor.
2.Maintain and update regularly the Property/Equipment Cards for each class of fixed asset.
